phase.name: Move draft plan + revise verbs into loom

contract.goal: Wire planVerb and reviseVerb into cli/loom.ts as top-level verbs `loom plan` and `loom revise-plan`, using the existing verbless-namespace dispatch pattern (the same `doctor` uses). No changes to plan/revise handler bodies; no changes to loom's dispatch flow. Companion to D1, which moved the handlers into cli/verbs/plan.ts.
acceptance_criteria: cli/verbs/plan.ts adds two registries: `PLAN_VERBS = { plan: planVerb }` and `REVISE_PLAN_VERBS = { 'revise-plan': reviseVerb }`, following the `<NAMESPACE>_VERBS` sibling convention (DOCTOR_VERBS, PROJECT_VERBS, etc).
cli/loom.ts NAMESPACES adds `plan` and `revise-plan` entries with short purpose strings.
cli/loom.ts VERBS_BY_NAMESPACE maps `plan: PLAN_VERBS` and `'revise-plan': REVISE_PLAN_VERBS`.
cli/loom.test.ts adds two dispatch tests verifying both verbs route to their handlers (missing-args surfaces, not unknown-verb), modeled on the existing `dispatch: verbless namespace (doctor)` test.
`npm test` green at 468 tests (was 466 after D1; +2 new dispatch tests).

Smoke: `bin/loom plan <slug> --plan-file=… --interview-file=… --no-commit` and `bin/loom revise-plan <slug> --revision-file=… --rationale=… --no-commit` succeed end-to-end against a scratch project root, producing the same envelopes as `bin/draft plan/revise`.
rules_applied: CLAUDE.md: composition over configuration — reuse the verbless-namespace dispatch flow rather than adding a special path for plan/revise.
CLAUDE.md: keep it basic — the new registries follow the existing `<NAMESPACE>_VERBS` shape; no new abstractions introduced.
PLAN.md Phase 2 prose (lines 239-253): wire as top-level `loom plan` and `loom revise-plan`. PLAN.md lines 75-77 + 153-154 dictate the `revise-plan` verb name explicitly (not `revise`).
LOOM-CONVENTIONS verbless-namespace pattern: `loom <namespace> [args]` where the namespace IS the only verb.
disqualifiers: Touching plan/revise handler bodies — D1 finalized them.
Touching cli/loom.ts dispatch logic — verbless-namespace flow stays as-is.
Removing or breaking bin/draft access — the handlers stay shared.
Wiring any other loom namespaces — out of scope.

actions: Appended two named-export registries to cli/verbs/plan.ts: `PLAN_VERBS = { plan: planVerb }` and `REVISE_PLAN_VERBS = { 'revise-plan': reviseVerb }`. Handler bodies untouched.

Edited cli/loom.ts: added import `{ PLAN_VERBS, REVISE_PLAN_VERBS } from './verbs/plan.ts'`; added `plan` and `revise-plan` entries to NAMESPACES; added the same to VERBS_BY_NAMESPACE; added both to VERBLESS_NAMESPACES; trimmed a stale parenthetical comment that said `pr is the only unwired namespace as of Phase 2 close` (no longer true since Phase 4's pr wiring).

Edited cli/loom.test.ts: inserted two new dispatch tests — `dispatch: verbless namespace (plan) routes to planVerb (missing-args surfaces)` and `dispatch: verbless namespace (revise-plan) routes to reviseVerb (missing-args surfaces)` — modeled on the existing `dispatch: verbless namespace (doctor)` test, just before the `pr namespace wired` test.
Ran `npm test`: 33 test files passed, 468 tests passed (was 466 after D1; +2 dispatch tests), 0 failures, ~620ms.

Smoke-tested `bin/loom plan loom-smoke --plan-file=… --interview-file=… --no-commit --pretty` against a scratch projects-root: exit 0, writes PLAN.md + INTERVIEW.md, auto-adopts loom substrate, emits `{slug, path, committed: false, loom_adopted: true}` — same envelope shape as `bin/draft plan`.

Smoke-tested `bin/loom revise-plan loom-smoke --revision-file=… --rationale=… --no-commit --pretty`: exit 0, writes revised PLAN.md with the `## Revision log` 2026-05-18 entry, emits `{slug, path, committed: false, rationale}` — same envelope shape as `bin/draft revise`.
Smoke-tested `bin/loom --help`: lists `plan` and `revise-plan` in the Namespaces section (alongside `doctor`).

Derived evaluator panel: `bin/guild derive-panel --files=cli/loom.ts,cli/loom.test.ts,cli/verbs/plan.ts` → evaluator-contract-fit, evaluator-react-api, evaluator-test-unit, evaluator-naming (fallback rules; same shape as D1).
Invoked /guild-validate with the four evaluators. All four returned `approved`. Aggregated verdict: approved, 0 blocking findings, 0 advisory findings, 0 conflicts.

First aggregation pass returned `flagged` with three parse-failures because my evaluator-output summaries put `VERDICT: approved` inline with prose rather than on its own line. Re-ran `bin/guild parse-and-aggregate` with the summary text reshaped (blank line before each `VERDICT:` line). This was a loop-input shape error, not an evaluator finding — no artifact changes were needed.

changes_since_previous: Second unit on this branch. D1 (checkin 01) moved the plan/revise handlers and removed `draft read`; D2 wires them into loom. The two PLAN_VERBS / REVISE_PLAN_VERBS registries are the only additions to D1's `cli/verbs/plan.ts`.
notes_for_pr: `bin/loom plan` and `bin/loom revise-plan` use the verbless-namespace dispatch flow (same pattern doctor uses). No changes to loom's dispatch logic were required — the flow already supported single-handler namespaces.

`revise-plan` (not `revise`) is the verb name. PLAN.md lines 75-77 and 153-154 dictate this explicitly: a top-level `revise` would have been ambiguous (loom's `project` namespace also exposes verbs that act on projects), and the explicit `revise-plan` makes the subject of the action obvious without needing context.

`bin/loom --help` formats `revise-plan` (11 chars) slightly off-grid: the `padEnd(8)` in `formatHelp()` produces two spaces between `revise-plan` and its description rather than the four-to-five spaces every other namespace gets. Cosmetic only; help text is still parseable. Out of scope for D2; a follow-up could widen the padEnd to 12 if the namespace list grows more long names.

Handler identifier `reviseVerb` keeps its existing name even though the verb at the CLI surface is now `revise-plan` rather than `revise`. The disqualifier rule against renaming the handler is intentional: it's a public identifier from D1's `cli/verbs/plan.ts` exports, and renaming it would touch D1's already-merged commit's diff for no behavioral benefit. The connection from `reviseVerb` → `loom revise-plan` is one grep away.
Both `bin/draft plan/revise` (D1's compat surface) and `bin/loom plan/revise-plan` (D2's new surface) work end-to-end with the same handler bodies. Phase 8 owns the eventual deletion of `bin/draft`.
